title: celtis aculeata var. laevigata
intro: Celtis aculeata var. laevigata, also known as the smooth spiny hackberry, is a variety of deciduous shrub or small tree belonging to the Celtis genus. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine, particularly for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of tannins and other bioactive compounds. The medicinal properties of Celtis aculeata var. laevigata have been associated with several health benefits, including the treatment of wounds, skin conditions, and gastrointestinal issues, as well as its potential role in managing fever and infections. Traditionally, the plant has been prepared in various herbal remedies, such as infusions, decoctions, and ointments, which are often used to treat a range of health conditions.
